title: Write Only Secret Settings Semantic Contract
description: Source-backed draft semantic contract for the write only secret settings capability, recovered from historical source-lineage evidence as lineage. Facets cite verified source-import manifest rows; proof is hermetic/runtime lineage, not fresh USF proof.
aiGuidance: Source-backed draft. Facet descriptions are concise source-backed summaries citing verified manifest rows; proof is hermetic/stale, not current readiness. Enrich facet detail and publish fresh proof before generation; respect the hexagonal constraint in ADR 0005.

lifecycle.description: Secret-bearing settings follow a write-only lifecycle: callers create, rotate, or update a secret, the value is validated before storage where applicable, then stored encrypted, hashed, or provider-masked, after which only non-sensitive metadata such as a has-secret flag and readiness is exposed. A blank or absent secret on update preserves the existing value, and clearing occurs only through the owning resource's lifecycle rather than via a raw-read path.

stateModel.description: State includes tenant admin credential lifecycle metadata, identity-provider secret presence with provider-masked values, secret stores for mail/webhook/API-key surfaces, and a token-crypto state distinguishing encrypted values from an explicit unencrypted test marker, all classified for readiness without ever materialising raw secret output. The persisted representation deliberately holds only protected or metadata forms of each secret.

permissions.description: Secret writes are governed by the permissions of the owning surface (such as auth-settings write, provider-config permissions, webhook write, and API-key create/revoke scopes), and reads return only redacted metadata. Secrets are tenant-scoped through their owning resource, and there is no permission that grants retrieval of a stored raw secret.

contracts.description: Contracts expose write-only secret fields on identity-provider create/update, credential rotation, mail-sender and webhook secret writes, and a reveal-once API-key create response, all mediated by a secret-store port with provider-backed and persistence-backed adapters. No operation in the contract supports later retrieval of a raw secret value.

validation.description: Secrets are validated before storage or provider mutation, unsafe URLs or configurations are rejected, invalid tenant credentials are classified before any store occurs, an empty identity-provider secret preserves the existing value, and encryption-key requirements are enforced for higher environments. Validation therefore runs ahead of any secret-bearing side effect.

errorModel.description: Outcomes are typed and fail-closed, including no-credential, invalid-credential, forbidden-realm-operation, realm-unreachable, generic validation failure, not-found, and provider-specific readiness states. Failures never echo secret material in their messages, so error handling cannot become a secret-disclosure channel.

auditModel.description: Audit records only safe metadata such as alias, the names of changed fields, a has-secret flag, a secret-changed flag, configuration key names, and readiness result. Audit is emitted before the secret-bearing provider or storage write, so a failed audit blocks the mutation and no raw secret value is ever audited.

readinessModel.description: Readiness reports configured, missing, invalid, forbidden, unreachable, or provider-unavailable states without exposing any secret material, and provider-backed secret stores require live substrate validation to be considered ready. The capability degrades to an explicit not-ready classification rather than assuming availability.

proof.description: Behaviour is exercised by unit proofs over token cryptography and audit-redaction plus runtime proofs of the secret-store contract and a provider-backed secret store against a live substrate, validating that only metadata is exposed and audited. Proof is hermetic/runtime lineage; fresh USF proof pending USF-101/USF-59.

uiSemanticDefinition.description: No verified UI source path in the manifest yet; author the UI semantic model before generation.
